title: Xtra Smoky Sichuan Pork Mu Shu
description: A smoky, dense, one-pot Chinese Sichuan dish featuring shoyu-koji, marinate, and low-fat-friendly ingredients, served in mu-shu form.
instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the pork shoulder and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the toasted Sichuan peppercorns, smoked paprika, shoyu-koji, soy sauce, and rice vinegar, and stir to combine.
Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer, covered, for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the chopped scallions and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Warm the mu-shu pancakes by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the mu-shu by spooning the pork mixture onto a pancake and serving immediately.
